verdict_reasoning: HSPD1/HSP60 is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin essential for folding imported proteins. However, an ecto-HSP60 pool is well documented on the surface of stressed and tumor cells, where it serves as a DAMP/innate immune signaling molecule and has been pursued as an immunotherapy target. Cell-death and infection contexts also externalize HSP60. This non-baseline surface display fits cell_state_induced. Not a classical TM/GPI protein, so not 'yes'; the documented stress/cancer ecto-pool rules out 'no'.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Magnitude and therapeutic tractability of the surface HSP60 pool vs. dominant mitochondrial localization is debated.
